region_of_origin: Singapore (served); Wagyu beef of Australian origin

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002725 covers beef burger as a food product; no specific Wagyu burger entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKM covers hamburger/beef burger category. No specific Wagyu variant code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 covers hamburger as a food concept; no Wagyu-specific code exists. Closest parent used.

preparation_method: served with burger sauce, gherkin, and salad

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one assembled Wagyu beef burger comprising a beef patty (~120 g), a ciabatta or brioche bun (~80 g), burger sauce (~20 g), gherkin (~10 g), and salad (~50 g), totalling approximately 280 g.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, ground, 80% lean meat / 20% fat, patty, cooked, pan-broiled (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy); Hamburger bun, plain (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy); composite estimation for assembled burger
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry for an assembled Wagyu beef burger exists. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of: (1) USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 174032 — beef ground 80/20 cooked patty for the beef component; (2) USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172793 — hamburger bun plain for the bun component; (3) USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 170918 — mayonnaise-type dressing for burger sauce approximation; (4) USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 168409 — pickles/gherkins; (5) USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 169247 — lettuce raw for salad. Wagyu beef is higher in monounsaturated fat than standard 80/20 ground beef; values are adjusted upward for fat and energy accordingly, consistent with published Wagyu nutritional literature (Gotoh et al., 2014, Meat Science). All per-100g values represent the assembled burger as consumed.

gi_source.title: Hamburger bun / white bread GI reference; mixed meal GI estimation for beef burger
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for an assembled Wagyu beef burger. GI is estimated as analogous to a standard beef hamburger on a white-flour bun. The University of Sydney GI Database lists white bread GI ~70 and hamburger bun GI ~61. The assembled burger meal GI is attenuated by the high protein and fat content of the beef patty and sauce, which slow gastric emptying and reduce postprandial glucose response. A composite meal GI of approximately 54 is consistent with published mixed-meal GI methodology (Brand-Miller et al., 2009, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition) and is classified as low (≤55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 55.4) / 100 ≈ 29.9, rounded to 29.8. Carbohydrate per serving = 19.8 g/100g × 280 g serving = 55.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg/100g; ~1176 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this burger as one serving provides approximately 1176 mg sodium, representing nearly 50% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; choosing a smaller portion or requesting reduced sauce can help lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~29.8 per serving) and refined carbohydrate from bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that one serving of this burger delivers approximately 55 g of carbohydrate with a moderate-to-high glycemic load of ~29.8; opting for a ciabatta bap over a brioche bun and pairing with a non-starchy salad may help moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.8 g/100g; ~13.4 g per serving) and cholesterol (~52 mg/100g; ~146 mg per serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should note that this burger provides approximately 13.4 g of saturated fat per serving, exceeding two-thirds of the recommended daily limit; however, Wagyu beef's relatively higher monounsaturated fat profile compared to standard beef may be marginally less atherogenic, and occasional consumption within a heart-healthy dietary pattern is preferable to regular intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Me Up is a gourmet beef burger served at Riverside Buns, Boat Quay, Singapore, featuring an Australian Wagyu beef patty as its centrepiece. The diner may choose between a ciabatta bap or a brioche bun to house the burger, which is dressed with burger sauce, gherkin slices, and fresh salad. Australian Wagyu beef is prized for its exceptional marbling, yielding a patty that is notably juicy, rich, and tender compared to standard ground beef, with a higher proportion of monounsaturated fatty acids. The assembled burger provides approximately 248 kcal per 100 g and around 694 kcal per full serving (~280 g), with 13.5 g protein, 13.2 g fat, and 19.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Sodium content is high at approximately 420 mg per 100 g (~1176 mg per serving), making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic load per serving is moderate-to-high (~29.8), driven primarily by the refined flour bun; the high protein and fat content of the Wagyu patty attenuates the overall glycemic response relative to a plain bun alone. Clinically, this burger is a moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its carbohydrate load, and a moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to its saturated fat content, though Wagyu's favourable monounsaturated fat profile is a partial mitigating factor. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those requiring halal-certified food without confirmation from the restaurant. It contains gluten (bun), eggs (sauce and brioche), and potential dairy (brioche bun). Priced at SGD 17, it is positioned as a premium fast-casual burger in the Singapore dining market.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for an assembled Wagyu beef burger exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central). Values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy component entries (beef patty, bun, sauce, gherkin, salad) with an upward fat adjustment for Wagyu marbling consistent with published Wagyu nutritional literature. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database reference values for hamburger buns and mixed-meal GI methodology. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Cuisine tag in input JSON is listed as 'Taiwanese', but the food itself is a Western-style Wagyu beef burger served at a burger restaurant (Riverside Buns, Boat Quay, Singapore). The cuisine has been corrected to 'Western / Burger' to reflect the actual food type. The 'Taiwanese' tag in the input likely reflects a platform categorisation error or restaurant chain origin and is not nutritionally or culinarily accurate for this dish. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite sources used for this estimation. 3. All other -1 values in per_100g (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g) have been populated with estimated numeric values from USDA SR Legacy composite sources where available; only vitamin_d_mcg remains -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 4. Halal status is unconfirmed; Wagyu beef from Australia may or may not be halal-certified depending on the specific supplier used by the restaurant. 5. The serving size of 280 g is an estimate based on typical assembled burger weights; actual restaurant portion may vary. 6. GI of 54 is classified as low per the Veritas threshold rule (≤55 = low). 7. The food_name has been normalised from the promotional menu name 'Beef Me Up' to the canonical English food name 'Wagyu Beef Burger'; the original menu name is preserved in aliases.
